title: pisum sativum subsp. syriacum
intro: Pisum sativum subsp. syriacum, also known as the Syrian garden pea, is a subspecies of the pea plant that is native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparation of Pisum sativum subsp. syriacum typically involves drying and powdering the seeds, which can be consumed as a supplement or added to herbal teas.
